primary_sources.title: Dismissal of Anwar Ibrahim as Deputy Prime Minister, 2 September 1998
Public Prosecutor v Anwar Ibrahim — Sodomy I and corruption charges, Kuala Lumpur High Court 1998-1999
Weiss, M. The Roots of Resilience — Reformasi mobilisation chs. 3-5 (Cornell, 2020)

notes: PM Mahathir sacks DPM Anwar Ibrahim on 2 September 1998 amid the Asian financial crisis policy dispute (capital controls vs IMF orthodoxy). Anwar mobilises 'Reformasi' street movement; arrested 20 September under ISA, beaten by police chief Rahim Noor (the 'black eye' image is iconic). Convicted on corruption and sodomy charges 1999. Direct consequence: founding of Parti Keadilan Nasional 4 April 1999 (later PKR), the multi-ethnic reformist party that anchors the modern PH coalition. The reform-vs-status-quo cleavage gains primary salience as a cross-cutting axis from this moment. Notes that the Anwar saga continued through Sodomy II (2008-2018), pardon 2018, PM 2022.
